explanation:
Automotive cost calculations are essential for vehicle owners deciding between electric and traditional fuel vehicles. Understanding fuel efficiency, maintenance costs, and total cost of ownership helps you make informed financial decisions.

## How the Calculation Works

This calculator multiplies your annual driving distance by the consumption rate for each vehicle type, then multiplies by the respective fuel/electricity price to show total annual operating costs. It provides a quick comparison to estimate your potential savings.

The formula is straightforward: **Annual Cost = (Annual km ÷ Efficiency) × Unit Price**

For electric vehicles: Cost = (km ÷ 100) × kWh consumption × electricity price
For gas vehicles: Cost = (km ÷ km/L) × gas price

## Important Notes

This calculator provides estimates for comparison purposes. Actual costs vary based on driving conditions, vehicle age, battery degradation, and regional energy prices. For critical financial decisions, consult with a professional. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)?
faq.a: TCO includes purchase price, fuel/electricity, maintenance, insurance, registration, and depreciation. Electric vehicles often have higher upfront costs but significantly lower operating and maintenance costs.

faq.q: Is charging at home cheaper than public charging stations?
faq.a: Yes. Home charging typically costs 30-50% less than public fast-charging stations. Check your local electricity rates for accurate estimates.

faq.q: How much do EV batteries degrade over time?
faq.a: Modern lithium-ion batteries typically retain 80-90% capacity after 10 years under normal use. Most manufacturers warranty batteries for 8-10 years.

faq.q: Are there tax incentives or rebates for electric vehicles?
faq.a: Many countries offer federal tax credits, state rebates, or registration exemptions. Research your location's incentive programs—they can significantly reduce EV costs.

faq.q: Are electric cars suitable for long-distance driving?
faq.a: Modern EVs can travel 200-400+ miles per charge, and charging infrastructure is expanding. Long trips require more planning than gas cars but are increasingly practical.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are completely free with no registration or login required. Use it as many times as you need.
Results are estimates using standard formulas validated internationally. Actual costs depend on driving conditions, vehicle age, and regional prices. For critical decisions, consult a professional.

faq.q: Do you store or track my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser locally. We never transmit your data to our servers or any third party.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
